rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH ANSANELLI ET AL VS. FORSYTHE GENERAL CONTRACTORS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion Calendar for December 11, 2024, line 1. PETITIONERS JOSEPH ANSANELLI, KATHRYN CORRO'S Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award. Off calendar. The moving party failed to provide courtesy copies in compliance with SF Local Rule 2.7B. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: MUSEUM PLAZA LLC, A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY ET AL VS. BERDING & WEIL LLP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses By Plaintiff Waterview Development Llc To Defendant'S Requests For Admission, Set Three; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery calendar for Wednesday, December 11, 2024, Line 3. 4 - DEFENDANT BERDING & WEIL LLP's Motion To Compel Further Responses By Plaintiff Waterview Development LLC., To Defendant'S Requests For Admission, Set Three. Defendant Berding & Weil LLP's Motion To Compel Further Responses By Plaintiff Waterview Development LLC To Defendant's Requests For Admission, Set Three is denied. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties). For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: DOUGLAS F CARLSON ET AL VS. PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Compelling Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents (Set One) Against Plaintiff Douglas F. Carlson; Request For Monetary Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery calendar for Wednesday, December 11, 2024, Line 4. DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY's Motion For An Order Compelling Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents (Set One) Against Plaintiff Douglas F. Carlson; Request For Monetary Sanctions. Continued to January 17, 2025. One of defendant's main arguments is that plaintiff waived all of the cited objections in plaintiff's untimely response to the production demand. On December 5, 2024, the court granted plaintiff relief from the waiver. Given this new development, the motion is continued. Defendant shall file a supplemental brief of no more than 7 pages to address this new development by December 23, 2024. Plaintiff to file a supplemental brief of no more than 7 pages by January 10, 2025. The parties are ordered to continue to meet and confer and apprise the court of any resolution in the supplemental briefs. For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: FERNANDO SALMERON REYES ET AL VS. ASN NATURAL STONE, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion Calendar for December 11, 2024, line 5. DEFENDANT CAESARSTONE USA, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Defendant Caesarstone USA, Inc.'s motion to strike is DENIED. At issue is whether Plaintiffs Fernando Salmeron Reyes and Mirabel Salmeron Aguilar's allegations could support a finding of malice, oppression, or fraud grounding a punitive damages award. (Civ. Code, 3294(a).) "[M]alice does not require actual intent to harm. [Citation.] Conscious disregard for the safety of another may be sufficient where the defendant is aware of the probable dangerous consequences of his or her conduct and he or she willfully fails to avoid such consequences." (Pfeifer v. John Crane, Inc. (2013) 220 Cal.App.4th 1270, 1299; see also College Hospital, Inc. v. Superior Court (1994) 8 Cal.4th 704, 713 ["For plaintiffs attempting to prove malice by showing a 'conscious disregard' of their rights as opposed to an actual intent to harm, the Act imposed additional requirements of 'despicable' and 'willful' defense conduct."].) The court must liberally construe the complaint. (See Code Civ. Proc., 452.) Here, Plaintiffs allege Defendant intentionally concealed known product dangers, and further allege officer, director and managing agent knowledge/ratification. (See, e.g., Complaint, pgs. 36, 54 and 103-156.) Plaintiffs' allegations are not conclusory. They are sufficient at the pleading stage to support their request for punitive damages. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)
